manifestNotes: All facts were checked against official museum, gallery, organiser, studio or brand sources on 28 June 2026. External imagery from official pages should be cleared through source-owner press permissions before live email distribution where required.

sources.storyTitle: Project a Black Planet: The Art and Culture of Panafrica
sources.imageCredit: Project a Black Planet installation view, 11 June - 6 September 2026. Image: Thomas Adank, Barbican Art Gallery.
sources.usageStatus: Official Barbican exhibition-page image embedded and browser-verified for preview. Confirm press/send permissions before live distribution.
sources.selectedBecause: The exhibition directly connects contemporary art, posters, film, journals, archives, photography and visual culture through Pan-Africanism.

sources.storyTitle: Japanese Women Photographers: From 1950s to Now
sources.imageCredit: No newsletter image used; official exhibition images and credits remain on The Photographers' Gallery page.
sources.usageStatus: Official source verified. No external image embedded.
sources.selectedBecause: A photography-led June opening with strong relevance to authorship, publishing, moving image and women-led visual histories.

sources.imageCredit: No newsletter image used; official Pavilion images and credits remain on the Serpentine Galleries page.
sources.usageStatus: Official source verified. No external image embedded.
sources.selectedBecause: A June architecture and public-space story with strong relevance to rhythm, material, shadow and bodies moving through image space.

sources.imageCredit: No newsletter image used; official exhibition images and credits remain on the Somerset House page.
sources.usageStatus: Official source verified. No external image embedded.
sources.selectedBecause: Escher's graphic precision and spatial illusion are useful references for composition, pattern and visual instability.

sources.storyTitle: adidas Originals and Coca-Cola: Originals are the Real Thing
sources.imageCredit: Official adidas Originals x Coca-Cola press image from adidas News.
sources.usageStatus: Official adidas News press image embedded and browser-verified for preview. Confirm press/send permissions before live distribution.
sources.selectedBecause: The campaign connects sport, streetwear, casting, summer light and World Cup-adjacent visual culture through a strong public-space image.
